codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of golden sample testing for procedure validation. The feature adds GoldenSample struct, GoldenSampleStore trait (SQLite-backed), GoldenSampleTester, and GoldenSampleService. All 37 tests pass, build succeeds, and clippy passes with no warnings.

issues.description: In validate_procedure(), when check_output_match() returns false, validation criteria are not evaluated (code skips to else branch). This is an intentional 'fail fast' approach but means criteria are not fully checked on output mismatch. This does not affect feature requirements since the procedure is still correctly marked as failed and flagged for review.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill procedure correctly - explored codebase structure, implemented module, iterated on compilation errors and test failures, verified with cargo build/test/clippy. No deviations from skill procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback shows followedProcedure: true with no deviations

sharedStateObservations.observation: Implementation follows existing patterns in swell-memory crate: async_trait for traits, Arc<SqlitePool> for pool, proper error handling with SwellError, chrono for timestamps. No violations of project conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: golden_sample_testing.rs uses same patterns as sqlite_store.rs and other modules in the crate

summary: Feature implementation is complete and correct. Worker created comprehensive golden_sample_testing.rs with 2047 lines implementing GoldenSample struct, GoldenSampleStore trait, GoldenSampleTester, and GoldenSampleService. All expected behaviors are implemented: procedures validated against golden samples before promotion, pass rate threshold (80%) enforced, failed procedures flagged for review. 37 tests cover all functionality. No blocking issues found.
